title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
description: Attackers inject malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes) to manipulate database queries, potentially gaining unauthorized access to data, modifying records, or even executing arbitrary commands on the database server.
attack_vector: Unvalidated user input used directly in SQL queries.
mitigations: Utilize parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent the interpretation of user input as SQL code.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization to filter out potentially malicious characters.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.
